name: messages
context: Send, update, delete, and schedule Slack messages in channels and DMs
info: This skill covers all message operations in Slack: posting new messages with chat.postMessage, updating existing messages with chat.update, deleting messages with chat.delete, and scheduling future messages with chat.scheduleMessage. Messages can include plain text, rich Block Kit layouts (see [[slack/block-kit]]), attachments, and thread replies. The @slack/web-api package provides the programmatic interface. All methods require a bot token with the chat:write scope.

sections.title: Send messages
sections.body: Use `chat.postMessage` to send messages to channels or DMs. Requires `channel` (ID, not name) and `text`. Optionally include `blocks` for rich layouts (see [[slack/block-kit]]). The response includes `ts` (timestamp) used as the message ID for updates, deletes, and threading.

sections.title: Update and delete messages
sections.body: Use `chat.update` with the original `ts` and `channel` to modify a message. Use `chat.delete` to remove it. Bots can only modify their own messages. Store the `ts` from the send response to reference later. Useful for updating status messages or removing ephemeral content.

sections.title: Thread replies
sections.body: Set `thread_ts` to the parent message `ts` to reply in a thread. Use `reply_broadcast: true` to also surface the reply in the channel. Threads keep conversations organized — [[slack/bots]] often reply in threads to avoid channel noise.

sections.title: Message formatting
sections.body: Slack uses `mrkdwn` syntax: `*bold*`, `_italic_`, `~strike~`, backtick for code. Mention users with `<@U0123456>`, channels with `<#C0123456>`, and broadcast with `<!here>` or `<!channel>`. Links: `<https://url.com|display text>`. For rich layouts beyond text, use [[slack/block-kit]].

sections.title: Message scheduling
sections.body: Use `chat.scheduleMessage` with a `post_at` Unix timestamp to schedule delivery. List pending scheduled messages with `chat.scheduledMessages.list`. Cancel before delivery with `chat.deleteScheduledMessage` using the `scheduled_message_id` from the schedule response.

scripts.name: Send Slack Message
scripts.description: Send a message to a Slack channel or DM using the Web API

channel.description: Channel ID (e.g., C0123456789) or user ID for DMs

blocks.description: Block Kit blocks for rich message layout

threadTs.description: Parent message timestamp for thread replies

depends.reason: Requires a Slack app with bot token and chat:write scope
